title: dopatrium angolense
intro: Dopatrium angolense, also known as the poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Dopatrium angolense has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including a tincture or decoction, which can be used to treat different ailments.
